signals.raw:
Trending markets** also cover broader topics like "Nasdaq round-the-clock trading by June 30?" among 166 live options with $3M+ volume, but no resolutions, announcements, or tips on imminent moves are confirmed as of April 21 data[1].

signals.headline: YES** on longer-term bullish predictions (e
signals.raw: YES** on longer-term bullish predictions (e.g., $10,000-$40,000 ETH by 2030), but **NO** on "up right now" or April resolutions moving markets—**LOW confidence** in any unconfirmed hype like April 18 YouTube claims of $6K (contradicted by price data).[1][2][3]

signals.headline: US Inflation Hits Two-Year High at 3
signals.raw:
US Inflation Hits Two-Year High at 3.3% in March | YES | HIGH confidence**

The Consumer Price Index for March 2026 surged to 3.3% year-over-year, the highest level in nearly two years, driven primarily by a **21.2% monthly spike in gasoline prices**—the worst single-month gas increase since 1967[1][3]. Headline inflation rose 0.9% month-over-month, substantially above the 0.2-0.3% range consisten

signals.headline: Market-Moving Implications:**
signals.raw:
Market-Moving Implications:**

The inflation spike has **dramatically delayed expectations for Federal Reserve rate cuts in 2026**[2][3]. Markets had previously priced in multiple rate cuts this year, but traders have now pushed back the timeline for the first cut and reduced the total number anticipated[2]. The Fed is holding its benchmark rate at 3.50%–3.75%, with most policymakers still expecti

signals.headline: Why This Matters for Prediction Markets:**
signals.raw:
Why This Matters for Prediction Markets:**

The core driver is the Iran war energy shock, which has persisted longer than initially expected[2]. While core CPI remained relatively contained at 2.6% year-over-year with a 0.2% monthly gain, the re-acceleration in core services inflation indicates that price pressures have spread beyond energy[2]. This fundamentally changes the Fed's policy calculus
